captured_from: Public AWS Bedrock Converse API docs (with Anthropic Claude foundation model).

examples.notes: Bedrock Converse API (Anthropic Claude): camelCase fields (inputTokens not input_tokens). The Converse API is provider-agnostic and DOES return totalTokens (Anthropic's native API does not). `cacheReadInputTokens` → cached_tokens; `cacheWriteInputTokens` → cache_creation_tokens. Note: no top-level response_id in Converse; the AWS request id lives in HTTP response metadata (not the body) — no leaf to label. No `model` field either (Converse echoes the modelId in the request, not the response body).
